sections.paragraph:
ἐμβάπτω 1 aor. ἐνέβαψα (s. βάπτω; Hipponax 36; Aristoph., X. et al.; Hymn to Demeter in PSI 1282, 60; TestZeb 4:9)=prec. entry dip (in, into) τὶ ἔν τινι Mt 26:23; cp. J 13:26b P66 et al. Mid. dip for oneself (Aristoph. Fgm. 151 [in Athen. 9, 367b]) abs. (sc. the hand, or what the hand holds) Mk 14:20 (cp. Athen. 6, 345e). βάψας τὸ ψώμιον … ἐμβάψας τὸ ψωμίον J 13:26 v.l.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hipponax Hipponax , VI b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

prec. prec. = preceding

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

abs. abs. = absolute

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 321
